MOTU UltraLite-mk3

Section-based notes for routing, CueMix, converters, monitoring, and hardware setup.

  • Overview
  • Hybrid FireWire/USB2
  • Mic/Guitar Inputs
  • Comprehensive I/O
  • Mixing and effects
  • CueMix FX software
  • Classic reverb
  • Modeled analog EQ
  • Vintage compression
  • Tuner & analysis tools
  • Clock and sync
  • MIDI
  • Expansion & drivers
  • Feature Summary

Classic Reverb

Source: MOTU product page

Classic Reverb™ is a DSP-powered hardware reverb built into the UltraLite-mk3. With decay lengths up to 60 seconds, it covers everything from tight room ambience to vast cathedrals — entirely in hardware, adding zero CPU load to the computer.

Parameters

  • Room Type: selects the reverb character — room, hall, plate, chamber, etc. Each type has a distinct density and reflection pattern.
  • Size: scales the apparent room dimensions from a small booth to a large concert hall.
  • Pre-Delay: sets the time between the dry signal and the first reflection. Set pre-delay to 20 ms or more to create a sense of space between the vocal and the reverb tail — keeps the initial attack clear.
  • Decay: how long the reverb tail sustains before fading. Short decay (0.5–1.5s) for vocals in a tracking mix; longer decay (2–4s) for pads and atmosphere.
  • Diffusion: controls the density of the reflections. High diffusion = smooth, washy reverb tail; low diffusion = discrete, audible early reflections.

Using as a Send Effect in CueMix FX

In the Mix tab, each channel has a Reverb Send knob. Instead of inserting the reverb directly on a single channel, use it as a parallel send — multiple inputs (SM58 vocal, Hydrasynth bus) can share the same reverb simultaneously while the dry signal remains unaffected. This is more flexible and CPU-efficient than per-channel inserts.

Vocalist Comfort Reverb

During tracking, send a small amount of the SM58 input to the Classic Reverb in the headphone cue mix. Singers perform better when they can hear a little reverb on their voice — it relaxes the delivery. Keep the reverb in the headphone mix only; record the dry signal to Ableton for clean production-side processing.

Built for fast lookups, cleaner routing decisions, and practical studio recall.
